name: Active Directory DLLs Loaded by Office Applications
description: Detects highly suspicious image loads where Active Directory-related DLLs, specifically `kerberos.dll` or `dsparse.dll`, are loaded by Microsoft Office applications (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook). This is not a typical behavior for Office applications and may indicate a sophisticated attempt at authentication abuse, credential theft, or an advanced spearphishing attack.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{computer_name}**: the Microsoft Office application **{process_image}** loaded the highly suspicious Active Directory-related DLL **{image}**. This activity is a strong indicator of a **Spearphishing Attachment** attack (**MITRE ATT&CK T1566.001**) or an advanced attempt at **Credential Access**. Legitimate Office applications do not typically load these DLLs. Immediate action is required to isolate the host and perform a full forensic analysis to determine the source of the malicious document and the extent of the compromise. The event occurred under the user **{user}**.

parameters.name: process_image
parameters.description: The full path to the Office application that loaded the DLL.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The full path to the Active Directory DLL that was loaded.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process ran.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The hostname of the system where the event occurred.
